title: Harmony Hedgehogs
short_description: Two or three notes ring out together — is the chord happy (major), sad (minor) or spooky? Higher up, name the interval between the notes.
description: The Harmony Hedgehogs sing in little clusters of notes, and you train your ear on harmony — the way notes sound stacked together. Listen to each chord and decide if it's a bright, happy major, a gloomy, sad minor, or a spooky, unsettled sound; then in the top band, hear two notes and name the interval between them — a third, a fifth or a full octave. It teaches the building blocks of harmony beyond single notes: chord quality, consonance and the size of intervals. Every sound is synthesized live in the browser, no recordings. Grade bands ramp from happy-versus-sad, to happy-sad-spooky, to naming intervals. Correct ears and streaks earn bonus points. A free online chords, intervals and harmony music game for kids in grades 1–5, feeding your score and GP on OnlyGrins.
controls: Listen to the notes (tap replay to hear again), then tap the chord feeling or interval.
